commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: Human handling is needed for the protected maintainer label, missing live proof, failing current CI check, and canonical-choice decision; I did not find a narrow patch defect suitable for an automated repair lane.; Cleared: The diff changes OpenAI/voice-call runtime logic and tests only; it does not add dependencies, workflows, package metadata, secrets handling, downloads, or generated code. (sha=86d33e4f3b53c564de5e25b609289938245a7a67)
commands.review_summary:
The PR changes OpenAI realtime voice greeting/manual speech to use instructed `response.create` events, preserves deferred/retry response instructions, and adds voice-call/OpenAI regression tests.

PR surface: Source +50, Tests +352. Total +402 across 4 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for source-level reproduction: current main sends an explicit initial greeting while OpenAI realtime defaults Server VAD `create_response` to true, and the linked report includes repeated live-call reproduction. I did not run a live Twilio/OpenAI call in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: Human handling is needed for the protected maintainer label, missing live proof, failing current CI check, and canonical-choice decision; I did not find a narrow patch defect suitable for an automated repair lane.

target.title: fix(voice-call): avoid OpenAI realtime double greeting
target.body:
Fixes #85846

## Summary

- Avoid the voice-call plugin's duplicate OpenAI realtime greeting by making proactive/manual OpenAI speech an instructed `response.create` instead of injecting a synthetic user message.
- Keep outbound OpenAI calls proactive: the bridge still calls `triggerGreeting()` on ready when an initial message exists.
- Preserve configured session instructions for explicit/manual speech and carry the latest response intent through deferred, cancel, and active-response retry paths.
- Keep session instructions as the base voice instructions; initial greeting instructions are scoped to the first proactive response.
- Narrow the realtime test callback before invoking optional `sendUserMessage`, fixing the CI `check-test-types` failure on the prior head.

## Verification

- `CI=true pnpm check:test-types` -> passed
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/voice-call/src/webhook/realtime-handler.test.ts extensions/openai/realtime-voice-provider.test.ts --reporter=dot` -> 2 files, 74 tests passed
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check extensions/voice-call/src/webhook/realtime-handler.ts extensions/voice-call/src/webhook/realtime-handler.test.ts extensions/openai/realtime-voice-provider.ts extensions/openai/realtime-voice-provider.test.ts`
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxlint --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.core.json extensions/voice-call/src/webhook/realtime-handler.ts extensions/voice-call/src/webhook/realtime-handler.test.ts extensions/openai/realtime-voice-provider.ts extensions/openai/realtime-voice-provider.test.ts`
- `git diff --check refs/remotes/upstream-safe/main...HEAD`
- `git diff --check`

Signed head: `86d33e4f3b53c564de5e25b609289938245a7a67`.

Note: no live Twilio/OpenAI outbound call was run in this environment.
